text: not after I asked them to fight .” “And just what the hell do you think you’re going to do in there?” Cooper demanded . “Whatever the hell I can .” He stared at her, jaw clenched. “Shit,” he muttered and ran a hand over his face. “Nancy’s going to kill me, but she gets just as stubborn, and there’s no budging her once she’s made up her mind, either. Newton, get back there. Tell them what’s happening. If they can spare any more bodies, we might need them here .” “Check on Kate,” Lara said. “ Please .” Newton’s brow-plates drew together. “Thank you , Lara .” “ For … what ?” “For getting me out of that hole, even if it was indirectly. I shall never — ” “Just save it, okay? I’ll see you soon .” Despite his lack of skin, his expression was clearly troubled. “As you say. Soon , then .” Newton walked away. She watched him for a few seconds before Cooper caught her attention . “Take this,” he said, holding out another pistol. It was larger and heavier than the one Ronin had given her. “Two hands when you shoot. It’s going to have a hell of a kick, but I don’t want you to hesitate. Just…stay back. That clear ?” Lara tightened her grip on it and nodded . Cooper pointed to Chester. “Like the Colonel said. Like a fly on shit .” “Yes, Captain .” “Good. Now let’s move. We’re already late for the party .” Chapter Thirty - Five “G et that AMR up here, now !” Maul shouted . In Ronin’s periphery, a soldier scrambled forward with a long, black rifle — an anti-materiel rifle, Ronin’s data said. It didn’t come fast enough . Comp reached the doorway, shoulders clipping the edges of the entry and tearing it to pieces. The remaining glass shattered. Drywall and bricks rained down through the entryway. A cloud of dust obscured Ronin’s vision, but not enough to hide the makeshift barrier exploding with the force of the oncoming bot . The desk broke into countless pieces, and bent, mangled chairs flew in all directions. Ronin ducked out of the way, but at least two of the soldiers went down. The heavy rifle clattered to the floor . If the room had seemed cramped with all the soldiers packed within, Comp made it impossibly tiny. Its powerful arms swung, knocking a synth across the room and tearing down a portion of the ceiling. More dust and debris filled the air . Flashes of gunfire cast strange shadows in the haze, and the shouting from all sides blended into an indecipherable cacophony. Ronin scanned Comp for potential weaknesses, assessing every detail he could make out with limited visibility; the exposed cables of its neck were a potential weak point, but were at least lightly armored . Ronin leapt back from a swinging arm. At the edge of his optical field, the shadowy forms of gearheads charged through the devastated doorway. Stepping back, he turned toward them, raised his rifle, and shot into the haze, targeting their shadows. One of them slumped backwards, but another dark figure took its place . The AMR fired behind Ronin, its thunderous shot easily distinguishable from the other small arms, followed almost instantly by a metallic pang. Ronin returned his attention to Comp. The actuators in its left arm whined. Sparks crackled inside its forearm, illuminating a large hole in the metal . At the doorway, Maul and two other soldiers battled the invading gearheads with fists and firearms. An explosion just outside rocked the room. Bits of shrapnel bounced harmlessly from Comp’s casing, several pieces slicing into Ronin’s skin . A gearhead stumbled through the entry, everything missing from its abdomen up. Electric sparks and lubricant sprayed from its remains as they fell . Unfazed by its damage, Comp lunged forward. Ronin twisted to evade its massive fist, but his hip locked, halting his movement. The blow glanced off his temple. It sent Ronin to his knees, filling his optics with static for a microsecond . Despite his reinforced casing, the next blow would be Ronin’s end. While he’d been retrofitted for combat, he knew — though the data was older than his current awareness — that Comp had been designed for it. Comp was a walking tank, built for situations just like this; its purpose was never to guard doors, but to smash them in . Bringing up his favorite images of Lara — of her hair, and her eyes, and her impossibly alive smile — Ronin raised his rifle . The bouncer recovered from its swing, actuators audible as it prepared another. The window was brief. But just as Ronin didn’t want to exist in a world without Lara, he wouldn’t allow her to suffer a world without him . Creaking, Comp’s neck cables adjusted, pivoting its head back toward Ronin . The dustwalker’s CPU calculated the trajectories and he fired, twice, in a tenth of a second . Comp’s optics shattered . Ronin rolled aside clumsily. The massive hand came down, smashing floor tiles. Comp’s torso twisted, whipping its damaged arm in a wide arc that took down one of the overhead lights, and then it stepped forward, flailing . The AMR boomed. A new hole opened up on Comp’s chest plate, and the bot staggered, dripping lubricant and hydraulic fluid from the creases of its casing. Tendrils of smoke curled up from the narrow gaps around its neck cables . Gunfire cracked at the entryway . Throwing its arms up, Comp slammed its hands into the ceiling and pulled. A huge chunk of the ceiling tore off, falling atop Ronin and completely obscuring his vision. Comp’s heavy footsteps were audible over the chaos as it thrashed blindly . Ronin’s diagnostics indicated only minor damage to his skin. He clawed his way out of the debris, emerging in a room that looked nothing like it had
